title: Markthalle Neun - Quick Summary
items: **With Markthalle Neun, the day matters more than anything else.** The same Kreuzberg market hall is a food festival on a Thursday evening, a full weekly market on Saturday, a calm little shop on a Monday, and a **locked door on Sunday**. Pick the right day and you barely need to plan the rest.

**Street Food Thursday is the headline: every Thursday, 17:00 to 22:00.** Since 2013, small stands sell focused international food, from Korean buns to Peruvian ceviche to proper barbecue. It is the clearest proof that Berlin food is more than currywurst and kebab.

**The weekly rhythm:** Monday to Wednesday a small market (~12:00-18:00), Friday the Big Market (~12:00-18:00), Saturday the Big Market from ~10:00-18:00, and **Sunday closed**. Saturday morning is the best daytime visit.

**It is a real market in a beautiful old hall.** Markthalle Neun opened in 1891 as the ninth of Berlin’s covered market halls and was reopened by locals in 2011. Original green iron columns and a glass roof, permanent traders like the Kumpel & Keule butcher, plus weekly stalls.

**Getting there:** Eisenbahnstrasse 42/43, 10997 Kreuzberg. Take the **U1 to Görlitzer Bahnhof**, then a five-minute walk; a normal AB ticket covers it. Bring some cash, because not every stall takes cards.

**Best move:** treat a market day as a picnic-builder. Buy bread, cheese and a few things that look good, then eat by the canal. It pairs well with the nearby Turkish Market on Maybachufer (Tuesdays and Fridays).
